title: tylanthus willdenowianus
intro: Tylanthus willdenowianus, also known as Wild Tea Tree or Bois de Willdenow, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rutaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of the Americas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. Consuming the plant's leaves or roots has been reported to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and even acting as an antiviral agent. Tylanthus willdenowianus is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves or roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
